EX-10.10

classification.label: Return/Destruction
classification.rationale_short: Clause discusses content transfer encoding and content location

analysis.plain_english_summary: This clause appears to be a standard email header and footer, likely used for sending a document electronically.

why: The clause does not contain any language that would indicate a significant business risk.

norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider including a clear statement of the purpose of the email and the document being sent.

reasons: The clause is a standard email header and footer, but it may not be clear what the purpose of the email is or what the document being sent contains.

classification.label: Miscellaneous
classification.rationale_short: No clear contractual content, appears to be an exhibit title

clause.text:
CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT

 

This Confidentiality Agreement
(Agreement) is entered into on                    ,

analysis.plain_english_summary: This clause is a confidentiality agreement that outlines the terms and conditions for maintaining confidentiality.

why: The clause is a standard confidentiality agreement and does not introduce significant risk.

norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider adding a specific definition of confidential information.

reasons: The clause is a standard template, but the specific circumstances of the agreement may affect its applicability.

clause.text:
2021 by and between A SPAC I Acquisition Corp (the SPAC) and __Jin Ming_________ (Recipient).

 

WHEREAS, in connection with
a potential business relationship, the SPAC may disclose to Recipient certain Confidential Information (as defined below);

 

NOW THEREFORE, in consideration
of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:

analysis.plain_english_summary: The parties agree to keep confidential information shared between them.

why: The clause is a standard confidentiality agreement, which is common in business relationships.

comparison: This clause follows the standard format and content of a confidentiality agreement.

reasons: The clause is a standard confidentiality agreement, but the parties involved may have different expectations or interpretations of what constitutes confidential information.

clause.text:
1.
Recipient may receive or become aware of certain confidential and proprietary information relating to
the business and operations of the SPAC, A SPAC (Holdings) Acquisition Corp., the SPACs sponsor, any target company considered
by the SPAC for the initial business combination or any of their respective affiliates, including reports, financial information, trade
secrets, research information, finances and financial projections, current or future business plans and models and other business or operational
information (the 
Confidential Information
), regardless of whether such information is designated as confidential
information at the time of its disclosure. Confidential Information shall include all information (oral or written)
relating to the potential business relationship (including the fact that the SPAC has reached out to Recipient regarding the potential
business relationship and, to the extent applicable, has entered into such business relationship), that the SPAC furnishes or has furnished
to Recipient prior to, on or after the date of this Agreement, and all notes, analyses, compilations, forecasts, studies or other documents
prepared by Recipient that contain, reflect or are based on such information.

classification.label: Confidentiality
classification.rationale_short: Describes confidential information and its scope

analysis.plain_english_summary: The recipient may receive confidential information about the SPAC, its sponsor, target companies, and affiliates.

why: The recipient may be exposed to confidential information that could harm the SPAC or its affiliates if it is disclosed.

comparison: Standard confidentiality clauses typically do not include such broad definitions of confidential information.
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider revising the definition to include only information that is truly confidential and not publicly available.

reasons: The clause may be overly broad, but it is unclear what specific information is intended to be protected.

clause.text:
2.
Recipient hereby acknowledges the confidential and proprietary nature of such Confidential Information
and agrees that the Confidential information will be used solely in connection with Recipients service on the board of directors
of the SPAC and for no other purpose and further agrees not to disclose or otherwise attempt to use or personally benefit from any Confidential
Information that is disclosed to or known by Recipient until such time as the Confidential Information has become publicly available through
no action of Recipient, except to the extent required by law or as expressly permitted by the SPAC.

classification.label: Confidentiality
classification.rationale_short: Acknowledges confidential and proprietary nature of information

analysis.plain_english_summary: The recipient agrees to keep confidential information confidential and only use it for specific purposes.

why: The risk of unauthorized disclosure is low as the recipient is only allowed to use the information for specific purposes.

reasons: The clause is standard and widely used, but the specific circumstances of the agreement may affect its interpretation.

clause.text:
3.
If Recipient is requested or required, in connection with any proceeding by or before a governmental authority,
to disclose any Confidential Information, Recipient will give the SPAC prompt written notice of such request or requirement so that the
SPAC may seek an appropriate order or other remedy protecting the Confidential Information from disclosure, and Recipient will cooperate
with the SPAC to obtain such protective order or other remedy. In the event that a protective order or other remedy is not obtained or
the SPAC waives its rights to seek such an order or other remedy, Recipient may, without liability under this Agreement, furnish only
that portion of the Confidential Information which, in the written opinion of Recipients counsel, Recipient is legally required
to disclose, provided that Recipient gives the SPAC written notice of the information to be disclosed as far in advance of its disclosure
as practicable and uses its best efforts to obtain assurances that confidential treatment will be accorded to such information.

classification.label: Permitted Disclosures
classification.rationale_short: Clause describes when Recipient can disclose Confidential Information under certain conditions.

analysis.plain_english_summary: Recipient must notify SPAC of requests to disclose confidential information and cooperate to protect it. If a protective order is not obtained, Recipient can disclose only the required information.

why: The clause provides a framework for Recipient to disclose confidential information while minimizing the risk of unauthorized disclosure.

comparison: Standard confidentiality clauses typically do not specify the process for obtaining a protective order.
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider adding a specific timeline for Recipient to notify SPAC of requests to disclose confidential information.

reasons: The clause's effectiveness in protecting confidential information may depend on the specific circumstances of the request and the cooperation of the parties involved.

clause.text:
4.
In the event of the termination or conclusion of this potential or consummated relationship, Recipient
shall return or destroy all Confidential Information and destroy or permanently erase (to the extent technically practicable) all copies
of Confidential Information made by Recipient and use its reasonable endeavors to ensure that anyone to whom it has supplied any Confidential
Information destroys or permanently erases (to the extent technically practicable) such Confidential Information and any copies made save
to the extent required to be retained by applicable law, rule or regulation or by any competent judicial, governmental, supervisory or
regulatory body, or where the Confidential Information has been disclosed under paragraph 3 above.

classification.label: Return/Destruction
classification.rationale_short: Clause requires return or destruction of confidential information upon termination or conclusion of the relationship.

analysis.plain_english_summary: The recipient must return or destroy all confidential information and ensure that anyone else who has received it does the same, except where required by law or regulation.

why: Breaches of confidentiality can result in reputational damage, financial loss, and legal liability.

norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider adding specific timelines for return or destruction of confidential information.

reasons: The clause may not cover all possible scenarios, such as accidental disclosure or unauthorized access.

clause.text:
5.
Recipient acknowledges that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement
by Recipient and, in addition to all other remedies available under applicable law, the SPAC shall be entitled to seek specific performance
and to injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach.

classification.label: Remedies / Injunctive Relief
classification.rationale_short: Specific performance and injunctive relief mentioned

analysis.plain_english_summary: The SPAC can seek specific performance and injunctive relief if the recipient breaches the agreement.

why: The SPAC may not be able to fully recover from a breach, and injunctive relief can help prevent further harm.

comparison: Typically, remedies for breach are limited to monetary damages
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider adding a specific timeline for when the SPAC can seek injunctive relief

reasons: The specific performance and injunctive relief remedy may not be enforceable in all jurisdictions

clause.text:
6.
This Agreement shall be governed in all respects by the internal laws of the State of New York, without
giving effect to New York principles or rules of conflict of laws to the extent such principles or rules would require or permit the application
of the laws of another jurisdiction.

classification.label: Governing Law
classification.rationale_short: States the governing law of the contract

analysis.plain_english_summary: This contract is governed by the laws of New York, excluding any principles or rules that would apply laws from other jurisdictions.

why: The choice of governing law is a common practice in contracts, and New York law is a neutral jurisdiction.

comparison: Most contracts choose a governing law that is neutral or favorable to one of the parties.

reasons: The clause is a standard choice of governing law, but the exclusion of New York principles or rules may be subject to interpretation.

clause.text:
7.
This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original
copy of this Agreement, and all of which, taken together, shall be deemed to constitute one and the same agreement.

classification.label: Term & Survival
classification.rationale_short: Refers to the agreement's duration and continuation after termination

analysis.plain_english_summary: Counterparts can be used to execute the agreement

why: This is a standard clause in many agreements

reasons: The clause is clear and unambiguous, but may not be applicable in all jurisdictions

clause.text:
8.
This Agreement represents the entire understanding of the SPAC and Recipient and cannot be amended, supplemented
or otherwise modified except in a writing executed by the parties.

 

I have read and understand the foregoing terms
and conditions and agree to be bound by them as of the date written above.

 

[Signature pages follow]

 

[Signature page to confidentiality
agreement]

 

 

 

 

A SPAC I Acquisition Corp.

 

By:
 

 

 
Name: Claudius Tsang

 

 
Title: Chief Executive Officer

 

 
Date:

 

 

[Signature page to confidentiality
agreement]

 

 

 

 

 

 

Name: Jin Ming

 

Date:

 

 

[Signature page to confidentiality
agreement]

classification.label: Term & Survival
classification.rationale_short: Clause specifies the entire agreement and its modification requirements.

analysis.plain_english_summary: This agreement cannot be changed without a written agreement from both parties.

why: This clause limits the ability of the parties to make changes to the agreement, which may reduce the risk of disputes or misunderstandings.

comparison: This clause deviates from the standard practice of allowing parties to make changes to an agreement through oral or written communication.
norm_deviation.suggested_revision: Consider adding a provision for oral or written communication to make changes to the agreement.

reasons: The clause is clear and unambiguous, but the interpretation of 'writing' may vary depending on the jurisdiction.
